title: Find a shorter side (integer result)
problem: A right-angled triangle has a hypotenuse of $13$ cm and one leg of $5$ cm. Find the length of the other leg.

steps.instruction: Rearrange to find the unknown side $a$.
Take the square root to find $a$.

title: Find a shorter side (decimal result)
problem: A right-angled triangle has a hypotenuse of $11$ cm and one leg of $5$ cm. Find the length of the other leg, rounded to 2 decimal places.

steps.instruction: Write the rearranged form of Pythagoras' theorem.
Take the square root and round to 2 decimal places.

title: Multi-step: find a side then calculate perimeter
problem: A rectangle has a diagonal of $15$ cm and one side of $9$ cm. Find the perimeter of the rectangle.

steps.instruction: Recognise that the diagonal is the hypotenuse of a right-angled triangle with legs equal to the rectangle's sides.
Substitute the known values to find the missing side.
